_meta.description: Milestone data for the AWARE DPP Legislative Tracker
_meta.note: This file is the source of truth for DPP legislation. index.html currently embeds this data, but a future release will have index.html read from this file dynamically.
_meta.citation_guide: All refs must be primary EU sources. Type must be: official | technical | analysis | consultation

refs.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 — Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation (ESPR)
refs.detail: Official Journal of the European Union. Entered into force 18 July 2024.

refs.title: ESPR and Energy Labelling Working Plan 2025–2030 — Commission Communication COM(2025) 187
refs.detail: Adopted 16 April 2025. Identifies priority product groups (textiles, furniture, steel, aluminium, tyres, mattresses) and delegated act timelines for 2025–2030.

refs.title: Ecodesign Forum — Meeting Documents (CIRCABC)
refs.detail: Official meeting notes and working documents from the Ecodesign Forum, the stakeholder body consulted on all ESPR delegated acts and the Working Plan.

refs.title: Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 — EU Battery Regulation
refs.detail: Official Journal. Entered into force 17 August 2023. Repealed Directive 2006/66/EC from 18 August 2025. Covers carbon footprint declarations (Article 7), due diligence (Article 48), and battery passport (Article 77).

refs.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1252 — Critical Raw Materials Act (CRMA)
refs.detail: Official Journal. Entered into force 23 May 2024. Article 28 introduces permanent magnet traceability and passport requirements.

refs.title: Have Your Say — Digital Product Passport (DPP Registry)
refs.detail: European Commission consultation portal entry for the DPP registry initiative under ESPR Article 12.

refs.title: Have Your Say — Digital Product Passport Rules for Service Providers
refs.detail: European Commission consultation portal entry for DPP rules for service providers.

refs.title: CIRPASS2 Initiative — DPP Technical Pilots
refs.detail: Pre-industrial pilot project testing DPP data models, digital identifiers, and technical standards across product categories.

tracks.name: ESPR Framework
tracks.desc: The overarching regulation establishing the DPP framework for virtually all physical goods placed on the EU market. Specific data requirements arrive product-by-product via delegated acts.

milestones.title: ESPR Working Plan 2025–2030 adopted
milestones.note: Textile delegated act shifted to 2027 from earlier 2026 signal. Mandatory compliance now expected no earlier than 2028.

milestones.title: Unsold goods disclosure obligations — large enterprises
First delegated act consultations — textiles and steel (expected)
milestones.note: Date not yet confirmed in official Commission calendar. Multiple analyses indicate late 2025–early 2026. Watch Have Your Say portal.

milestones.title: Textile & footwear destruction prohibition — large enterprises
Textile delegated act formally adopted (est.)
milestones.note: Commission originally signalled January 2026. Current consensus across multiple analyses is 2027. This date will be confirmed when the consultation launches.

milestones.title: Unsold goods destruction prohibition — medium enterprises

tracks.name: Battery Passport
tracks.desc: The most advanced DPP track in the EU. Running under a dedicated regulation, separate from ESPR. Being closely watched as the template for all future DPP implementations.

milestones.title: Carbon footprint declaration — EV batteries
milestones.note: VERIFY: Confirm whether the Commission has published the implementing act specifying the carbon footprint calculation methodology. Expected mid-2025.

milestones.title: Commission adopts battery passport implementing act
Battery Passport mandatory — EV, LMT, industrial (≥2kWh)
Due diligence — cobalt, lithium, nickel, natural graphite
milestones.note: VERIFY: History of postponement on this article. Confirm current status of implementing acts.

tracks.name: Textile & Apparel DPP
tracks.desc: The textile-specific DPP track inside ESPR. Has its own JRC preparatory study timeline and stakeholder process. Most directly relevant track for fashion and apparel supply chains.

milestones.title: JRC preparatory study on textiles begins
Textiles confirmed as priority in Working Plan
Textile delegated act — public consultation (expected)
milestones.note: Highest-value document to capture when published. Watch the EC Have Your Say portal.

milestones.title: Textile delegated act formally adopted (est.)
milestones.note: Commission originally signalled January 2026. Current consensus is 2027.

tracks.name: CRMA Passport
tracks.short: Critical Raw Materials Act — Permanent Magnets
tracks.desc: Introduces traceability and passport requirements for permanent magnets. Relevant for electronics, EVs, wind turbines, and industrial motors.

milestones.title: Commission adopts permanent magnet labelling format
milestones.note: VERIFY: Check EUR-Lex for CRMA implementing acts adopted since November 2025 to confirm this has been published.

milestones.title: Permanent magnet passport mandatory
milestones.note: Applies from 24 May 2027 or two years after the relevant delegated act, whichever is later.
